description: Tuna Aguachile is a bold, refreshing, and spicy Mexican seafood dish that captures the vibrant flavors of the Pacific coast. Made with sashimi-grade tuna, lime juice, and fiery chiles, it’s a zesty, invigorating plate that’s perfect for summer meals or elegant appetizers. Unlike ceviche, this dish is flash-marinated, preserving the silky texture of the tuna while delivering an unforgettable punch of flavor.
ingredients: [{"name": "sashimi-grade tuna", "unit": "oz", "amount": "8"}, {"name": "freshly squeezed lime juice", "unit": "cup", "amount": "1/2"}, {"name": "serrano or jalapeño chiles", "unit": "pieces", "amount": "2-3"}, {"name": "fresh cilantro leaves", "unit": "cup", "amount": "1/4"}, {"name": "cucumber", "unit": "piece", "amount": "1/2"}, {"name": "red onion", "unit": "piece", "amount": "1/4"}, {"name": "Salt", "unit": null, "amount": "to taste"}, {"name": "garlic clove (optional)", "unit": "piece", "amount": "1"}, {"name": "avocado slices (optional garnish)", "unit": null, "amount": null}, {"name": "microgreens or additional cilantro (optional garnish)", "unit": null, "amount": null}]
instructions: Prepare the Marinade: In a blender, combine lime juice, chiles, cilantro, and a pinch of salt. Blend until smooth. (Add garlic if using.) Slice the Vegetables: Thinly slice the cucumber and red onion. Set aside in a bowl of ice water for extra crispness if desired. Marinate the Tuna: Place the tuna in a shallow bowl or plate. Pour the marinade over the tuna, ensuring it’s well coated. Let it sit for 5–10 minutes—no longer, to preserve its raw, silky texture. Assemble: Drain the onions and cucumbers and pat dry. Arrange them on a serving platter with the marinated tuna. Spoon extra marinade over the top. Garnish and Serve: Add optional garnishes like avocado or microgreens. Serve immediately with tostadas or tortilla chips.
notes: Variation Ideas: Try a rojo version with dried chiles, or add mango for a fruity twist. Canned Tuna Option: Drain well, flake gently, and chill in marinade for 10–15 minutes before serving. Diet-Friendly: Gluten-free, low-carb, keto, paleo, and dairy-free. Pairing Tip: Serve with a light Mexican lager or citrusy cocktail like a mezcal margarita.
tags: Mexican, seafood, spicy, appetizer, main course, gluten-free, keto, paleo, dairy-free
